Chen Chen nodded. “Next, X, make arrangements for Shaw to be an instructor of subspace navigators. Let him pass on to them his experiences in subspace navigation. We need to cultivate more subspace navigators... Besides, I’ve heard that he still has his parents?”

“Yes.”

Little X replied, “He sneaked into Gambia, Africa after he murdered people in a fit of rage and lost contact with his parents. His parents still don’t know anything about him.”

“Then, let him return to Oceania to visit his family.”

Chen Chen said decisively, “It’s such a waste to not return home after one has made something of oneself. I think he should be missing his parents too. For him to work for us more loyally, make it happen.”

“Got it, leave it to me.”

Little X replied.

...

Shaw came to the residence arranged by Blacklight Biotechnology — a single-family villa located at Walvis Bay Lagoon in the Eco Science City.

This was a medium-sized villa with an area of ??3,000 square meters. There were three living rooms, six bedrooms, and more toilets than the number of bedrooms.

In addition, the villa also had a huge courtyard. One end of the courtyard extended directly into the lagoon, making the villa a picturesque, high-end sea view residence.

“This place...”

Shaw looked at all this, mouth agape and dumbfounded. “Is it for me?”

“Yes, this ocean view villa is completely yours, Mr. Winfred. This is the company’s transfer agreement and this is your real estate certificate.”

A middle-level manager of Blacklight Biotechnology spoke with a hint of envy in his eyes, but he still smiled and said, “However, according to the contract, you cannot resell the villa. If one day you don’t want to live here anymore, you can only sell ??it to the company, and the company will buy the villa again at one-half of the market price.”

Shaw nodded. Indeed, almost everyone who got to live here was a high-level executive of Blacklight Biotechnology. Since this was the core hinterland of Blacklight, it was natural that they would control the influx of outsiders and not allow private resales.

As for it being bought back at only half the market price, Shaw also understood. Land in today’s Eco Science City was worth its weight in gold, and Walvis Bay was the core area of ??the Eco Science City because of its beautiful scenery.

He could imagine the frightening price that a lot like this could fetch. Moreover, it would not have many buyers. The company was already being generous in reclaiming the property at half the market price.

“In addition, the company has issued a document. The position we have assigned to you is... Oceania Branch Company Supervisor.”

The manager opened the cover of another document and immediately displayed an astounded look. “This... Mr. Winfred, this is the first time I’ve heard of this post. From what it says, your role seems to be to supervise the senior executives of the company in Oceania to see if they are corrupt or accepting bribes.”

“What?”

Shaw raised his head abruptly at this, an incredulous glint in his eyes. “But...”

“I hear that you still have family over there?”

The middle-level manager guffawed. “It seems that the company is very considerate toward you.”

“But! But my identity in the outside world is still a fugitive!”

Shaw quickly explained.

“I know your previous status, you were a Delta-level subject after all.”

This middle-level manager was also a member of God’s Forbidden Zone. Now he proudly said, “But this is nothing. Your current position is higher than mine. So even if you used to be a fugitive in Oceania, it doesn’t matter. After all...

“The person to be arrested is a fugitive named Shaw Winfred. What does it have to do with you, the supervisor of Blacklight Biotechnology’s Oceania Branch?”

Hearing what the other party said, Shaw was stunned.

“Hahaha, it seems that you haven’t recovered from this surprise.”

The manager smiled kindly. “I have done everything they asked me to do and I’ll be going off now. Mr. Winfred, I wish you a happy move!”

With that, he turned around and left the courtyard of the villa, leaving Shaw who was still in a daze to soak in this wonderful news.

“I, I am free?”

Shaw raised his slightly trembling hands and held up the documents in disbelief. Even if they had transferred this villa to himself, it would not have made him so overwrought, but he did not expect that this company would give him such a huge surprise...

Thinking of this, Shaw’s heart churned with a hundred emotions. He used to resent Chen Chen for making him risk his life, but at this time, that resentment had long since vanished like smoke, and only deep gratitude remained in his heart.

“Father, Mother, it’s been four years... How are you doing?

“I can come home...”

chapters.title: Chapter 638: Hunting Down Consciousness Stealer
chapters.body:
Chapter 638: Hunting Down Consciousness Stealer

Chapter 638: Hunting Down Consciousness Stealer

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

“Finally! We’ve finally done it!”

Thousands of players gathered at the peak of the mountain and looked down at the depthless pit beneath their feet, each of them utterly thrilled at what they had achieved.

“Oh, there’s a system notification saying that I received the ten thousand dollars bounty, are you guys getting this too?”

“I got it too, I can finally buy the monster truck I’ve been eyeing!”

“I got it too, haha!”

“The main mission is finally out of the way. Speaking of which, what’s with this mission anyway, it’s so nonsensical! If the game is based in the end times, why do we have these futuristic gadgets?”

“What’s this device shaped like a hammer?”

“Haven’t you read the mission overview? This is the device used to block psionic channeling. The setting of this game is that psionicists only exist in the Holy Fusion Empire while regular players don’t have access to psionic abilities, so we need to use this device to suppress these psionicists!”

“It’s not just us, this is a server-wide mainline mission. I’ve logged into the private forum analyzing the in-game statistics and they’ve revealed that more than three hundred guilds are performing the same mission, only in different locations!”

“Our mission is considered easier compared to the rest. Some guilds need to install these reality anchors deep inside the territory of the Holy Fusion Empire. Those missions are usually composed of tens of thousands of participants at a time and they duke it out with the people of the Holy Fusion Empire for several days!”

“It’s that intense?”

“Bravo I say, bravo!”

“Hey look, something is glowing down there!”

“Is this the sign of the device working properly?”

“Let’s get out of here...”

...

It was evening. Chen Chen, with his arms folded in front of him, stood by the edge of a valley, overlooking a seemingly endless stretch of the Pacific Ocean.

The setting sun painted the sky with a blood-red texture while the ocean mirrored the sky above. Far off in the distance, one could make out bare silhouettes of lonely islands shrouded in dense mist. Perhaps at this moment, people were setting up camps on the beaches and gazing back at Chen Chen’s direction as well.

The sun sank to the west. The glow of the setting sun shone over Chen Chen and cast a deep shadow to his side. His shadow then became the middle point where the golden afterglow of the sun merged into the deep red of the ocean. It painted a picture otherwise only seen in stories...

Drawing the perspective closer, the salty sea breeze gently caressed Chen Chen’s skin and made his clothes flutter. His dark figure remained still like a statue, his eyes closed and resting.

Chen Chen breathed softly and felt the world around him move.

“Sir Commander, after months of battle, all guilds have deployed reality anchors to their corresponding positions.”

X-112’s voice came through the earpiece. The tone of her voice was bright and crisp with a subtle note of excitement. “Shall we start the mission now?”

“Do it.”

Chen Chen answered softly.

Immediately after the order was given, an invisible wave suddenly unleashed somewhere far away. It traveled near the speed of light and Chen Chen quickly sensed a numbing sensation running across his body. The feeling was similar to being trapped and surrounded by quicksand. He felt bound and restricted by a suppressing force.

This was the effect of the reality anchor in its surroundings, a unique energy oscillation that suppressed psionic power. Only psionicist could feel its oppression.

Unlike the psionic energy suppression in the past, Chen Chen sensed that this one was significantly stronger. This was because it was the combined effect of three hundred and sixty-five reality anchors set up globally!

This was quite unlike the setup used by the mysterious civilization Chen Chen encountered where there were twenty reality anchors, each supported by a steel pillar nearly a kilometer tall. The design incorporated by Chen Chen was only several hundred meters long, but the total quantity was more than fifteen times that of the mysterious civilization!

According to Little X’s calculations, the combined effect of these reality anchors could rival that of the one Chen Chen encountered in the DOOM dimension, perhaps even surpassing it. It was more than enough to completely suppress the Consciousness Stealer.

Months of work led to what was seen today.

The psionic presence in the surroundings was completely suppressed, Chen Chen tried to evoke his psionic power but failed to produce anything. It felt like the source of the power was bound by an enormous force. He could not sense even the slightest drop of power spilling out.

If it could have this level of effect of Chen Chen, surely the same could be said of the Consciousness Stealer, right?

Chen Chen wondered. Half a minute later, bubbles began bursting along the surface of the ocean. These were not any ordinary bubbles, but enormous formations more than ten meters large. They exploded upon reaching the surface and they kept coming again, and again, and again...

In an instant, it seemed as if the ocean was boiling as the stream of gigantic bubbles continued rising from the bottom!

Chen Chen turned his attention to the depths of the ocean below his feet. A behemoth almost the size of an island seemed to be breathing underneath. It threatened to break through the surface.

The Consciousness Stealer was surfacing...

“Rumble...”

More bubbles rushed to the surface. At the same time, there was a surge of powerful air current accompanied by violent gushes of cold air! As the wind roared, a colossal beast larger than an island slowly rose from the depths of the ocean. The moment it reached the surface, the ocean boiled!

“It’s here!”

Chen Chen looked on in fascination as the “island” slowly rose. It was a thousand meters tall, then two thousand, three thousand...

The mountain continued to expand unendingly and in the blink of an eye, the upper half of the Consciousness Stealer grazed the clouds above. This was only its upper half while its lower half remained basked inside the ocean that seemed more like a shallow pool in comparison to its humongous size...

“Hum-!”

When the Consciousness Stealer opened its mouth positioned at the center of its chest opened, it emitted a deep roar that made the air tremble. The air quaked to such an extent that it carried faint ripples of its destructive powers that collided into a layer of barrier Chen Chen set up around him before disappearing.

Chen Chen used Field to create a vacuum space around his body to remove all oxygen content around him.

“Alosorthoth, it’s been a while!”

Chen Chen greeted with a faint whisper. He let his arms down and beheld the sight of the otherworldly monstrosity larger than anything the world had ever seen. He could see his figure reflected in the millions of eyes grown on the Consciousness Stealer’s body...

The sheer appearance of the Consciousness Stealer was something that was beyond human comprehension. If anybody else that was not Chen Chen, be it an Alpha-level psionicist or a veteran general of a vast army saw this monster, their sanity would break instantly. They would be reduced to a madman forever haunted with the sheer horror induced by the cosmic monstrosity...

This was the true terror of the Consciousness Stealer. Having a number advantage over it was entirely meaningless. Even with psionic abilities which was the strongest weapon in mankind’s arsenal against it, it could only leave negligible scratches on it...

“Roar!”

The Consciousness Stealer seemed enraged when it realized its shockwave ripples did not affect Chen Chen. Enormous tentacles emerged from its body and launched themselves at Chen Chen at supersonic speed!

There were at least several kilometers between Chen Chen and the Consciousness Stealer. In just a split instant, gigantic tentacles shaped like great pillars seemed like they were about to smash Chen Chen and threaten to obliterate him with their sheer weight!

“So, you’re resorting to physical attacks now that you can’t use your psionic abilities?”

Chen Chen taunted with a quick flick of his eyebrows. “Too bad for you, physical attacks are useless against me!”

Just as the tentacles reached several hundred meters away from Chen Chen and it seemed there was no way out, they were ripped into innumerable shreds! They seemed like inferior substances composed of fragile sand and gravel when approaching Chen Chen and were torn into specks of dust instantly!

This was the destructive prowess of Chen Chen’s Field that imposed its effects on a molecular level. At its maximum range, everything that entered within a-hundred-and fifty-meters radius of his body would be completely broken down at the most basic molecular level. From an outsider’s perspective, it seemed like they were broken into powder!

“All you have is raw strength but you don’t know how to use it properly, what a shame.”

Chen Chen remarked blankly. He stood on an invisible platform in the air and slowly approached it step by step. “Maybe I won’t be your match if we’re sparring with psionic energy, but things are different now. Your enormous body looks like a giant bullseye to me!”

chapters.title: Chapter 639: Dawn
chapters.body:
Chapter 639: Dawn

Chapter 639: Dawn

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

After taunting the Consciousness Stealer, Chen Chen’s figure vanished from his original point and before the Consciousness Stealer could realize what was happening, he appeared right next to it. He summoned several bolts of turquoise-colored missiles from his palms and punctured them into the monster’s body!

“Thump!”

Giant splashes of black-colored blood splattered everywhere as Chen Chen penetrated its body!

“Hrrr-!”

The Consciousness Stealer howled with a mix of pain and anger. More tentacles appeared inside its body to try to claw Chen Chen out but they were obliterated into specks of dust and mist before they could reach their target. No matter how much it tried, it could not reach the tiny, feeble-looking human!

“Boom!”

After about five minutes, a gigantic hole was torn in the back of the Consciousness Stealer and Chen Chen emerged from it, then took to the skies above!

“Phew, you sure are hard to kill...”

Chen Chen exhaled, his eyes seemed to glow with excitement as he said, “Even with my destructive powers, it’ll be hard to kill this thing completely. Good thing I have the nanorobots to help me.”

At the drop of this mention, black mists slowly seeped out of the torn openings of the Consciousness Stealer’s body. The cloud of mist seemed to possess lives on their own as they could be seen pouring back into the Consciousness Stealer after only just emerging.

The Consciousness Stealer kept swiping at this mist helplessly before resorting to wrapping its body with its tentacles. When it realized that its attempts were futile, it began tearing off chunks of flesh from itself as a defensive measure. Still, a large amount of black mist poured out of every crevice of its body. They clung to its body stubbornly like maggots drawn to rotten flesh!

One minute, five minutes, ten minutes...

One hour, two hours, three hours...

Chen Chen stood above the Consciousness Stealer from dusk until midnight and watched it struggle. After enough time passed, its body seemed hollowed out and large peels of skin began tearing off. They tore off along with large columns of dark smoke sprouting out of its body. These clouds of smoke were darker than the night itself!

In the end, the Consciousness Stealer stopped resisting. The tentacles stopped struggling and rested lifelessly on the ocean as its body began to sink!

“Bang!”

When the body of the Consciousness Stealer collapsed, it made a tremendous splash on the surface of the sea. The emerging black mist lost its vitality upon touching the water. Only the black mist remaining inside the body of the Consciousness Stealer continued its efforts of eating away at its body.

These black mists were the nanorobots.

Several decades of research by Blacklight Biotechnology had produced several machines of nanorobots. There were nanorobots purposed for corroding metallic substances and there were also some optimized for carbon-based life forms or plastic. These nanorobots were bionic structures like microscopic ants. They would devour substances and convert them into resources so they could duplicate more copies of themselves. These nanorobots could replicate themselves endlessly until their program was terminated...

They could go on endlessly until they had destroyed everything on the surface of the earth...

With this invention, Chen Chen could destroy the Consciousness Stealer without a trace of its remains.

Chen Chen silently watched as the bloated corpse on the ocean slowly shrunk under the effect of the black mist. He waited all night and by the time the sun crept up the horizon on the next day, the final chunk of flesh of the Consciousness Stealer had corroded without a trace.

The Consciousness Stealer was erased. Not a single cell of it remained to prove it once existed on Earth as they were all swallowed by the nanorobots.

“Alright X-112, lift the psionic restriction.”

Chen Chen ordered, “I’m going to use my psionic powers to detect if there’s any trace of it remaining..”

Yes, Sir.”

X-112 replied dutifully and deactivated the reality anchors, immediately lifting the global psionic suppression. The moment this was done, Chen Chen felt a surge of power rushing into his head.

“This is...”

Chen Chen’s expression changed immediately.

The surge of power looked like it finally found a crack it could slip through. Like a tiny hole poked into the bottom of an overflowing container, it first slowly seeped into Chen Chen’s mind. Soon, the pressure built up and it began overflowing through the crack until the dam broke open and they poured through the opening!

“Boom!”

A limitless surge of psionic energy poured into Chen Chen’s mind like a flash flood. This was the remnant of the Consciousness Stealer following its death that was held stagnant due to the reality anchor’s effects. The moment X-112 unbottled the reality anchor’s effects, the built-up psionic concentration flowed into Chen Chen’s mind unsparingly!

“Ah-!”

Chen Chen wrapped his hands around his head and screamed harrowingly while he suffered the torrent of psionic energy!

It was so overpowering that at one point Chen Chen considered getting out of this place. It was only a momentary thought while the sane part of his mind convinced himself to stay here and take in all of this psionic concentration!

One minute, two minutes, three minutes...

At first, Chen Chen counted the seconds and minutes that passed. As this went on, the sharp throb in his mind became so unbearable that he could not remain conscious anymore. His body and mind gave away. He tumbled from the sky above and into the ocean, creating a splash as he sunk into its depths!

“Glug, glug, glug...”

The seawater came over Chen Chen’s eyes and drowned out his consciousness. In the void of his mind, he saw something that glowed with a faint yellow. There was nothing in the world like it. It was surrounded by gray air currents with streams of blood-red bubbles gurgling around it. Then, something that he could not comprehend whether it was a sentient being or just a mere substance appeared amid this void.

Chen Chen’s consciousness brushed past these bubbles obstructing his path and looked into the center of a vast blackhole-like entity in the dancing void. Thousands of bright golden spots swirled inside the black hole!

The black hole along with its multitudes of beams of light flickered in the unending void with a gigantic ball of light at its center. The radiant ball of light outshone all the spots of lights hovering nearby it. It was the dazzling morning sun lighting up the night sky!

While Chen Chen was still trying to comprehend what he was seeing, the sun began to expand... It seemed like it was fueled by an unknown source of energy and with one giant surge, it doubled in size, then tripled, quadrupled!

Eventually, the sun exploded like a supernova would. It turned into a radiant burning meteor moments before its eruption and scattered into countless spouts of flames. The shockwave of its explosion scattered far and wide across the void...

“Could this be...”

Chen Chen finally realized what he was looking at, but his consciousness faded away after that.

After several minutes that seemed like an eternity. After what felt like several lifetimes of sleep, Chen Chen finally regained consciousness. He woke up on the seabed.

“Splash!”

Chen Chen torpedoed out of the surface of the sea. The seawater that clung to his skin and his clothes did not evaporate but instead had the atoms of their existence erased by his Field.

“That took far longer than I expected.”

Chen Chen muttered.

“Sir Commander, you’re finally awake!”

X-112’s urgent voice came from the earpiece. “What happened to you just now? I’ve been calling for you for more than ten minutes! I was going to mobilize our forces and send a team underwater to rescue you!”

“Nothing much, just claiming some ‘remnants’ that rightfully belong to me.”

Chen Chen answered, “I’ve been scattering the psionic seed to all of human society to bolster my powers. It looks like the Consciousness Stealer is doing that same thing. That explains why it needs humans alive. Anyway, it has become feed for me despite its struggles...”

Chen Chen raised his right hand and looked at his palm. “In the past, I absorbed the psionic energy from the Consciousness Stealer through the power of the ‘Consciousness Battlefield’. That was when I gained a part of Consciousness Stealer and in a way, was when I became the Consciousness Stealer.

“Now that I’ve killed this ‘twin’, its powers belong to me since we came from the same source...”

Chen Chen slowly pushed outward with his palms and created a powerful blast that launched into the sky and broke through the clouds, then went further onward to the ozone layer until it broke through into space!

“This power...”

Chen Chen looked at the gigantic pit formed in the sky with his psionic powers. He was floored by this display of power. “What terrific power!”

“Congratulations, Sir Commander!”

X-112 was the first to congratulate him upon witnessing this, “Sir Commander, you’ve once again raised your power level!”

“I see...”

Chen Chen could not stop himself from breaking into a smile when X-112 stated this. His smile grew from a subtle grin to an ear-to-ear smile until eventually, he began howling with laughter!

“Hahahaha....

“Hahahaha!!!”

chapters.title: Chapter 640: Showdown And Negotiation
chapters.body:
Chapter 640: Showdown And Negotiation

Chapter 640: Showdown and Negotiation

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

Like removing a hammered nail, Chen Chen’s worries scattered like clouds giving away to the radiant sun after absorbing the Consciousness Stealer.

From here on, dominating this world would be an easy task.

If Chen Chen wanted, he could swoop in and reign over this world all by himself, but this was not his current plan. Currently, there were ten million players in this and the average number of active users at any given time reached up to a million. It was only a matter of time before the total player base reached a hundred million.

That being the case, it was necessary that a common enemy existed to extend the lifespan of the game.

This was where the Holy Fusion Empire played a vital role. However, even without the Consciousness Stealer, the people of the Holy Fusion Empire still retained their psionic gifts. This alone would cause the war between the Holy Fusion Empire and the Joint Resistance Army. The battle between technology and psionic powers could drag on forever.

There was only one solution...

With this in mind, Chen Chen slowly shut his eyes. When he opened them again, they emitted a dazzling glow!

The moment he opened his eyes, a shining golden ray illuminated everything in sight!

“Zoom!”

Both eyes became miniature suns within their sockets. They cast their divine glow over the vast ocean and basked everything in the world in their divine ray!

Everything only lasted for an instant. Their light extinguished within a second and Chen Chen’s eyes could be seen reverting to their ordinary state.

“I’ve uprooted all of the ‘psionic seeds’ existing in this world. From here on, psionic abilities no longer exist in this world.”

Chen Chen breathed the cold air, sensing the subtle change in the atmosphere. “Now, the players can do whatever they please...”

After that, Chen Chen’s figure vanished with a mighty boom that resounded across the ocean, leaving behind a gigantic column of water sprout in his original location.

Chen Chen traveled at a speed thirty times the speed of sound and returned to the Blacklight City within fifteen minutes. Then, he left the end dimension using the time machine...

When Chen Chen uprooted the “psionic”‘ seeds earlier, something unimaginable and utterly nightmarish happened in the Holy Fusion Empire of the end dimension...

“Praise the Almighty Father, Lord Alosorthoth...”

In the capital of the Holy Fusion Empire, there was a monumental church that occupied more than half of the city center of the city. An old priest whose corrupted skin was almost peeling off his bones kissed the ground and repeated cultish chants with burning fanaticism, “You’re the lord over our destiny. You hold the essence of death and life. You’re the song of life, the river of time. We come before you as your loyal servants, may you give us limitless strength...”

Behind the old priest was an entire congregation of worshippers kneeling with their heads on the ground. Judging by the fabric of the robes they wore, this gathering of worshippers was not any ordinary crowd. Each of them was the great priests of the empire that traveled to the capital from their respective cities. Each one of them was honored priests and bishops befitting the symbolic black robe.

Their faces were covered in ulcers that made them look like victims of radiation exposure. Despite their appearance, one could distinctly tell that there was powerful psionic energy surging within their bodies.

In the real world, their psionic level would at least be tiered at Gamma- or Beta-level, some of them perhaps grazing Alpha-level.

Each and every one of them knelt on the ground like peasants. Positioned at the center of this formation was a giant bronze statue of the Consciousness Stealer resting on an elevated platform.

“Lord, you grant us the blessing of the fusion, we worship you at your throne, here we are, here we are...”

The elderly priest at the head of the congregation led the hymn. When he sang the words “here we are”, he suddenly felt the words caught in his throat and was hit with a sense of impending doom. He could not find the words to describe this sensation, but he could feel the vitality of his life draining away. He looked back at the other worshippers and wanted to call for help, but immediately noticed that they were making the same horrified expression as he did.

“Could it be that our God...”

A possibility came to the elderly priest. Only a second later, blood drained from his face and the last of his strength escaped his body. Then, life went out of his eyes...

“Thump!”

The elderly priest collapsed on the ground. Although his heart continued beating and blood continued flowing in his veins, the breath of life was gone...

The archbishop of the Holy Fusion Empire, who stood at the pinnacle of power, was dead.

All of the powerful priests of the Holy See accompanied the archbishop in death. It seemed as if they were struck by an instantaneous plague that wiped them all out. By the time the common servants who were charged with redundant tasks like oiling the lamps and cleaning the temple tiles regained their senses, they were met with this horrific scene.

This was not an isolated incident only bound to the capital of the Holy Fusion Empire. Everywhere around the Holy Fusion Empire, psionicists collapsed without warning no matter where they were, who they were, and what they were doing at the hour. The storm arrived unannounced and struck all the psionicists in the empire dead with no hope of saving them.

The psychic abilities manifested by the Consciousness Stealer in them were harvested mercilessly by Chen Chen in a split instant.

These people were the cornerstones of the empire. They were the nobles, generals, and descendants of high lords. They were the ones who oversaw the operation of the Holy Fusion Empire and with their deaths, the empire was brought to ruin!

Without warning, the entire empire was plunged into unsalvageable chaos...

...

When Chen Chen returned to reality, he received a notice from Little X.

“Sir Godfather, the members of the Blacklight Foundation request an audience with you...”

Little X informed, “Several minutes ago, I received the invitation for a Lilith Assembly from them. It seems that they joined forces and are plotting something.”

“I suppose it has something to do with the reality anchor?”

Chen Chen asked with a disinterested tone. It was an undeniable truth that he had employed the help of the players to set up more than three hundred reality anchors. Even if he wanted to conceal this, it was only a matter of time until the Earth Federation found out about it since the player would discuss it on the forums.

“It’s not only about the reality anchors.”

Little X shook her head. “They are beginning to have their suspicions about the Ancient Civilization, to be exact... They have had their suspicions for a long time. It’s only after the recent events that transpired that finally made them decide to confront you about it together.”

“This was going to happen sooner or later.”

Chen Chen chuckled. “Tell them I’ll be there, it just so happens that there are some things I’d like to discuss with them as well.”

After several minutes, Chen Chen sat down at his office. The environmental holographic simulator transformed the appearance of his office into a grandiose conference room decorated with a bright chandelier above.

The office table transformed into a roundtable with four camouflaged figures surrounding the roundtable.

“05, you finally decided to show up...”

The four members of the Lilith Assembly who were occupied with their private conversation immediately stopped their discussion when they saw Chen Chen appearing. They turned to him in unison and 03 was the one who spoke first, “So, enlighten us. What’s Blacklight Biotechnology planning with Ancient Civilization?”

“What are we planning?”

Chen Chen responded with a puzzled look, “It’s just a common product to make some quick money. All of you here are important individuals governing hundreds of millions of people. What’s there to worry about a small side business I’m running?”

“Ahem...”

01 emitted a dry cough and stated, “Chen Chen, I mean...05. Forgive me, I’m new here. This is my first time attending the Lilith Assembly but I’ve had my share of experience with the Blacklight Foundation even before I was appointed to the role of 01 so I’m familiar with your operation. Based on what I know, Blackwatch never embarks on useless business ventures. Just like in the past when you planted your roots in Africa, everyone thought that you were crazy for choosing such a poor location, but you proved everyone wrong by carving a blazing path and surpassed the Earth Federation...”

“You flatter me.”

Chen Chen smiled pleasantly.

“Whether it’s the military industry, space travel or outer space colonization, you have an established, strong vision for everything you involve your company in.”

01 continued. “I know that you’ve done this with the inheritance from your predecessor, but that’s a topic for another day. What we want to talk about today is the game you released. We had our engineers look at the game chamber you recently developed and found that a majority of the parts inside are nothing that we’ve ever seen. We also notice a jarring absence of certain necessary parts in common gaming equipment...

“We don’t know what your plan is, but one thing we know for sure is that this game you call Ancient Civilization is more than it lets on.”

02 chimed in. “I agree with 01. The gameplay provided by the game chamber is indistinguishable from reality. The in-game physics shown are extremely realistic and shouldn’t be achievable by today’s game engine standards. It’d require at least hundreds of millions of lines of code to simulate an in-game physics engine of that kind, which we couldn’t identify anything like that in the game chambers which you developed.”

04 added. “We had our specialized departments research the game thoroughly to figure out its backend structure. Based on the findings reported, it’d seem that the in-game world isn’t a virtual world at all... When our team used microscopes and other similar equipment on samples collected in the world, they reported the discovery of common bacteria and cell structures such as atoms and molecules on surfaces of in-game objects. Are you telling me that you’ve manufactured an in-game system so detailed that you included the cellular structures of all objects in the game?”

“That’s not all...”

01 said, “Our team managed to find a secret factory inside the game and managed to sneak inside after many attempts. They found countless corpses lying inside the factory which were transported to various spawn points scattered across the in-game world. It seems that after arriving at the spawn point, these corpses would suddenly spur to life. It appears that these are the avatars controlled by the players... If this is just a game, would you explain this unexplainable, intricate system to us?

“There’s more...”

They started to bicker and talk over each other, eager to tell Chen Chen about the problems of Ancient Civilization.

“Your point being?”

After they were all finished with their piece, Chen Chen motioned for a suggestion. “If you think Ancient Civilization isn’t a game, what is it? Dimensional travel?”

“We need you to answer that for us, 05.”

03 sounded calm. “We want to know the inner workings of Ancient Civilization and its true nature as well as... What your goal is.”

Chen Chen shook his head upon hearing of this and produced a sigh. “Well, since you’ve confronted me like this, I shall tell you the truth. What I can tell you is that Ancient Civilization is more than a simple game, just as you suspected. The game world as you call it is a parallel dimension – a world set in a parallel timeline, only with slight deviations from our current timeline!”

Chen Chen paused to allow for a moment to reflect. “I’m sure you’re familiar with what a parallel dimension is. It’s similar to the Rosen Bridge in the Indian Ocean which leads to the world of Resident Evil. Ancient Civilization is another parallel world formed from our encounter with the Consciousness Stealer.”

“I see...”

The mystery was unraveled and the members of the Lilith Assembly seemed relieved to finally uncover the truth. They had considered this possibility once, but could not be certain of it until Chen Chen directly confirmed it himself.

Now that they had verifiable proof from Chen Chen himself, the setting of the game suddenly made sense. The fusion plague that mirrored the fusion plague that once happened in the current world, the four major continents, and the remains of mankind. Suddenly, there was a plausible explanation for everything seen in Ancient Civilization.

“The game chamber is just a device used to transfer the consciousness of the player to another body placed in that dimension. In this sense, the game chamber serves as a transmission point that allows the players to control the body in another dimension remotely.”

Chen Chen explained, “The reason I launched Ancient Civilization as a game is to take advantage of the volunteering players to expand the dimension. This is my true goal.”

“What about the reality anchors?”

03 enquired, “The reality anchor is Blackwatch’s tactical weapon from what I understand. Why did you deploy more than three hundred of them in that dimension?”

“To suppress the planet’s psionic force.”

Chen Chen answered without skipping a beat, “I’m sure you’re familiar with the background of the world. It’s a world conquered by the Consciousness Stealer. In the past when we barely defeated the Consciousness Stealer in our current world, it was only in its infant stage. In that dimension, the Consciousness Stealer has fully matured and has become far more powerful. There’s only one way for us humans to defeat it.”

“To suppress its psionic potential and kill it while it’s weak?”

03 shot up from his seat and seemed astounded by the mere idea.

“That’s right and I’ve already done it.”

Chen Chen shrugged and declared boldly, “This is all I can tell you. There’s no need for you to be cautious of what I do with the game anymore because I’ve already achieved my goal. It doesn’t matter if you want to shut down the game or send more members to reverse-engineer it. It doesn’t bother me at this point.”

The four of them could hardly believe their ears when they heard Chen Chen making such a bold claim.

“I know what you’re going to say next.”

Sensing their reaction, Chen Chen spoke before any of them could, “The game chamber’s most useful feature is its quantum communication technology. This is the technology that enables it to transfer the player’s consciousness without any delay. Although this technology is still in its developmental stages, I wouldn’t mind sharing it with all of you so we can refine it together.”

“What?”

They were dazzled by this offer. Each one of them had committed their resources to research quantum communication technologies, only to no avail as of yet. They could not ask for more if Chen Chen would publicize his research findings.

“This is very unlike you.”

03 hesitated and probed, “We all know there’s no such thing as a free meal. What do you want in return?”

“I believe you’ve seen the urban central management system in Eco Science City and Ares Science City?”

Chen Chen smirked. “My request is very simple. You only need to implement the ‘Skynet system’ and the ‘digital judge’ developed by yours truly into your city structure!”

chapters.title: Chapter 641: Wang Xi Appears
chapters.body:
Chapter 641: Wang Xi Appears

Chapter 641: Wang Xi Appears

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

Nobody knew what Chen Chen discussed with the four members of the Lilith Assembly.

After the meeting, despite their reluctance, they agreed to implement Blacklight Biotechnology’s “Skynet system” and “digital judge” in certain specific cities for demonstrative purposes.

The so-called “Skynet system” was a project that utilized an artificial intelligence system to replace human-oriented law enforcement. First, ultra-high-definition cameras layered with many features which included facial recognition would be installed all over the city and ensure that not a single blindspot was left.

This included private companies, shops, streets, elevators, and corridors in residential buildings. All surveillance of public areas would be connected through a series of cameras and the combined feed would be relayed to the AI. Upon detection of criminal activities, there was no need for the victim to contact the police since the AI would notify the related agencies automatically and immediately.

This was the basis of the Skynet system.

Meanwhile, all of the court cases and trials were entrusted to the worthy hands of the “digital judge” artificial intelligence to analyze and hand out judgments systematically. All of the procedures involved were presented intelligently and transparently. This way, the governmental hierarchy could not intervene with the process. Unless the offenders could somehow influence the digital judge, this system would effectively bring an end to practices such as court favoritism, bribing, and other corrupt activities.

The only way to overturn the decision of the digital judge was if several high-level officials joined together and raised valid arguments in favor of the accused.

The reason why Chen Chen wanted to implement the digital judge was partially due to the looming threat of subspace. Based on his previous conversation with the three consciousnesses in subspace, he found that human beings or any intelligent creatures were the most vulnerable to the influence of subspace.

The subspace was a mirror of reality and reflected the many thoughts, emotions, and intentions of intelligent beings. It was a place where these combined factors manifested and became magnified.

Chen Chen determined that this was how subspace threatened reality, by imposing its ill intentions on the intelligent beings of the real world.

Which was why he saw it fit to speed up his plans and use whatever advantage he could to implement the Skynet system and accelerate the coming of the third era – the Skynet era which would cultivate a more civilized and orderly civilization. A well-structured, disciplined society was a powerful deterrent against the effects of the subspace.

Everything Chen Chen was doing now was to lay the foundation. Once the foundation had been set, everything he had envisioned should run its due course over the next few years.

“We’ve already paved the path for the third era. Everything should work out in time.”

After exiting the meeting, Chen Chen considered the matters again. It was then when he received another piece of information from Little X.

“Sir Godfather, I found Wang Xi...”

“Professor Wang Xi?”

Chen Chen was startled by this. “You mean to say that you found tracks of Professor Wang Xi online?”

“Yes.”

Even Little X sounded doubtful of this discovery. “According to my original database, Wang Xi should’ve completely vanished off the face of the earth due to the antimeme. The only reason I even know of the false existence of this person was that you were the one who told me about him. The strange thing is that earlier, I suddenly noticed Professor Wang Xi’s information lying idly in my database like it had been there all along. This included all of his information such as his childhood, studies, work, family background, and so on!”

“Which means that Professor Wang Xi has returned?”

Chen Chen flung himself out of his seat and motioned for the virtual display to appear in front of him. There were countless rows of data and graphical information on the screen, all of them were directly related to Wang Xi. All of them surfaced out of nowhere.

“Wang Xi is currently in Haizhou, Central Continent. He’s giving a lecture at Jiaotong University.”

Little X spoke like she could not believe the validity of the information she was uttering, “This must be him, I’ve hacked into the Jiaotong University network and confirmed that he’s scheduled for a lecture today. He’s giving a lecture as we speak. He’s the professor of the Chinese Academy of Sciences teaching the freshman biology students...”

Chen Chen was not going to let the moment go to waste. He left the office immediately and went to the teleportation room of the Spire Experimental Base, then teleported to the professor’s location using the Gantz black sphere.

“Boom!”

There was a tremendous explosion when Chen Chen took off at supersonic speed. He soared at thirty times the speed of sound and made for the city center at breakneck speed. The closer he got to his destination, the heavier his expression became. His solemn expression was mixed with great caution.

At this point, he decelerated to only several times the speed of sound and began his slow descent. By the time he reached the skies of Jiaotong University, he had almost come to a complete stop.

Chen Chen looked at the familiar university that had only recently been renovated and landed from above. Resting at the center of his palm was a levitating black item. Its mysterious powers made it so that the crowd of people circling back and forth around the campus did not notice him descending from above.

After landing, Chen Chen turned his attention to a building nearby. He considered for a moment, then took his first step and began advancing toward the building.

At last, Chen Chen arrived at the door of a classroom.

The door was made of sandalwood that was only recently sanded to give it a mint condition. After so many years, it seemed like everything had changed and yet not much changed at the same time. Chen Chen extended his arm outward and pushed the door gently.

“Creak!”

The door obliged and opened.

Hidden behind the door was the familiar sight of a large multimedia classroom. The seats were arranged along several elevated levels with stairs leading up to each row. The seats faced toward the lowest section of the classroom, where there was a holographically projected podium, meeting up to modern standards. A gentle-looking old man was giving his lecture with a warm, inviting smile.

“There’s a limit to the lifespan of men. We all go through phases of being born, getting old, becoming sick, and dying. Can somebody explain why we go through these processes?

“As we all know, the unit of life is cells. Each one of us here is the divided result of a tiny, fertilized egg cell... However, this division isn’t infinite.

“Theoretically, human cells can only divide up to 56 times. According to the calculation of the cell division cycle, the lifespan of a person should be around 120 years old. This theory is called...

“Why don’t we have our classmate over there diving under the table to answer this question?”

“Hahahaha!!”

The students erupted with laughter. A young man whose cheeks had turned beet red from embarrassment emerged from under the table and rose awkwardly before stammering, “Is, is it the Hayflick limit?”

Chen Chen remained at the entrance of the classroom and watched this scene unfold quietly. The present scene overlapped with his memories and put him in a sudden state of trance. He could not differentiate whether this was a dream or reality...

“Correct, please sit down!”

The old lecturer gestured kindly for the boy to sit down before continuing. “However, science and technology are advancing with each passing day and various advancements that can prolong life have been developed. The most iconic one of them all is, of course, the ‘age-reversal treatment’ technology.

“With our current technology, the effective lifespan of men has been extended between two to four decades and has reached more than a hundred years. We expect to see this rise up to a hundred and twenty years old very soon. The Hayflick limit has been overcome, but this isn’t the limit...

“We humans can live longer than this!

“Technological modification, genetic modification, and psionic modification... Of these three options, the one I see the most potential in is none other than psionic modification. To sum it up, each and every one of you seated here belongs to the generation that is closest to achieving the dreamlike immortality!

“There’s so much of the truth of the universe that is still waiting to be discovered by humans. Perhaps achieving immortality will be the first and most important step in the pursuit of the ultimate truth...

“In any case, the future is in your hands. Alright, that’s enough for class today!”

The ringing school bell echoed the lecturer’s dismissal only moments after.

“Clap, clap, clap...”

The students applauded and thanked the lecturer for the lecture before packing up their belongings and shuffling out of the lecture hall.

They shuffled out of the only exit in the lecture hall and would intentionally curve around Chen Chen as if driven by an invisible force, like a stream of river splitting around a boulder. They did not even look at Chen Chen at all. He stood in the center of the stream completely invisible and yet declared his presence like an invisible obstacle...

After all the students left, only two figures remained in the lecture hall.

One of which was Chen Chen.

The other was an old lecturer, still smiling – Wang Xi.

chapters.title: Chapter 642: Showdown
chapters.body:
Chapter 642: Showdown

Chapter 642: Showdown

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

Wang Xi had a kind, inviting smile that could light up an entire room. This was the effect he had on people. However, Chen Chen returned the old lecturer’s smile with a blank, stoic look.

“It’s been so many years and you still haven’t changed your script.”

Chen Chen took two steps forward, all the while fixing his eyes on the old lecturer who single-handedly altered the course of his life. “I’m sure this is the same sermon you gave when Zhao Shanhe was present.

“Am I wrong, though?”

Wang Xi suddenly erupted with laughter. “Chen Chen, you truly lived up to my expectations. Even those three rascals in the subspace couldn’t corrupt you. I must say I’m very pleased!”

“If you’re so pleased, why don’t you tell me what your goal is.”

The professor’s compliments failed to soften Chen Chen, who continued speaking with an indifferent tone, “You left the dimension key that presented infinite possibilities to me. You were the one who steered mankind to the subspace. The moment I discovered that it was your doing, you vanished with the power of antimemes and now, you made an unannounced reappearance. Would you tell me what it is that you’re planning?”

“That’s a long story...”

Wang Xi pretended to be startled by Chen Chen’s claims. Then, he sighed dramatically., “Before I answer your question, allow me to tell you a story.”

Wang Xi noted Chen Chen’s subtle frown but still went on. “This story started eons ago.

“Do you know billions upon billions of years ago, the subspace is a stagnant place, nothing like the form it assumes today. Even back then, some of the oldest civilizations were already thriving in the Milky Way.”

Wang Xi narrated a story of an ancient age, far beyond the comprehension of any humans, “Among these civilizations, there were three distinct classes that stood above the rest of their peers. One of them was an energy life form, present since the birth of the universe. They fed on stars to sustain their existence but despite their impressive strength, they were unable to develop a unique civilization of their own.

“The other two superior classes, if you’d believe it, were fragile physical beings. One of them was the first to traverse beyond the stars and galaxies. They developed astounding technology in their wake that could change the structure of time and space and allowed seamless weaving in between dimensions. They were also the first to realize the potential of psionic abilities to a point where the technology they developed had become interwoven with their psionic powers...

“This was a carefree, well-natured civilization that was willing to achieve the greater good for all. Their status was akin to a mentor in the scale of the galaxy.

“However, their contemporary was a separate civilization cursed by the stars they were born from. The conditions of the planet that housed them were so hostile that this civilization was cursed with an extremely short lifespan from birth, and an even higher infant mortality rate. Despite the odds, they managed to advance their technology to an unbelievable level and yet they could not find a way to nullify the inherent corrupted state of their natural DNA from being born on this star...

“The cursed state of their being had made them grow into a hard, paranoid, and spiteful species. The composition of their body was also rejected by the subspace, causing them to not be able to harness the power of psionic abilities. This civilization was an extreme opposite of the other civilization mentioned previously.”

Wang Xi continued his tale. “Sixty million years ago, this cursed civilization and the mentor civilization finally crossed paths. When the cursed civilization realized the blessed conditions the mentor civilization was born with, in addition to their near-limitless lifespan, it aroused the flames of resentment within them.

“That was the start of a galactic war... The cursed civilization launched an unprovoked attack on the mentor civilization. As stated previously, they were unable to harness psionic abilities, therefore could not utilize any subspace-related technology. The mentor civilization capitalized on this and used the subspace navigation available to them to traverse across each corner of the Milky Way. This allowed them to subdue the cursed civilization that could only travel at sub-light speeds.

“As you’d expect, the cursed civilization fought an uphill battle and were soon cornered to their birth planet. Despite the unfavorable odds, their hard and bitter species refused to admit their defeat. They continued their valiant research to seek a solution to defeat their enemies.”

Wang Xi suddenly paused for a moment, a gloom appeared in his eyes. “At last, there was a turning point...

“The cursed civilization’s efforts had finally paid off when they made a grand discovery – they discovered the energy life form that existed since the birth of the universe and realized that it was this intergalactic species that had been consuming the energy emitted from their planet all along. They were the reason the planet had become so hostile to its inhabitants.

“With their backs against the wall, the cursed, despicable species made a pact with these energy form beings. They were willing to construct physical bodies for these beings and worship them and in exchange, they would be granted immortality so they could triumph over the mentor civilization.”

Chen Chen displayed a pensive look when he heard the tales spoken by Wang Xi. “Are these energy form beings stronger than these two civilizations you claim to be at the peak of the Milky Way?”

“Yes.”

Wang Xi was proud to make this declaration, “These energy form beings were divine beings with an unending source of strength, they were close to Godlike in the realm of the real world!

“In the end, the energy form beings who were naive to the wretchedness of the cursed civilization agreed to these terms. Soon, they obtained the high technology metallic shells designed by the cursed civilization. They transferred their consciousness into these shells and in these shells, they were immortal, immune to the effects of aging and disease...

“From there on, the cursed civilization and the energy form beings joined forces and launched an attack on the mentor civilization together and finally overwhelmed the opposition. The mentor civilization enlisted the help of many of its allies of different planets to their cause but in the end, their efforts were in vain. By the end, they were nearly entirely wiped out!”

Professor Wang Xi sighed. “However, the energy form beings were finally brought to ruin by their naivety. The cunning cursed civilization deceived them and spurred a civil war among their midst. The energy form beings had fallen into a trap as they failed to realize that the cursed civilization had been known for their paranoid and wretched character all along... These godly beings were betrayed and crushed into smithereens, then sealed away...

“An extreme minority of these energy form beings managed to escape the massacre.”

After finishing his tale, the professor shut his eyes briefly and summarized, “Do you understand now? The reason the subspace has become such a hostile dimension is directly connected to this ancient war. The corrupt and wretched emotions swept across the subspace, the endless mass of killing, bloodshed, and death changed the subspace over time until it became what it is today.”

“Which means that the subspace is susceptible to the intense emotion generated by war, violence, superstition, and such?”

Chen Chen remarked and enquired further, “What became of this cursed civilization in the end? It doesn’t seem like they continued to govern the galaxy.”

“They’ve entered a deep slumber.”

The gentle smile of Wang Xi slowly subsided and turned into a watchful, alert frown instead. “They’ve sustained major losses during the ancient war as well. To preserve their bloodline while preventing the birth of another civilization similar to the mentor civilization, they devised a plan for their eventual return. They rest in the subspace and if in the eventual future a planet crumbles to the subspace invasion, this cursed civilization will awaken from their eternal slumber and continue their mindless slaughter.

“This is the story I’ve been meaning to tell you, an ancient story spanning to the beginning of time.”

Wang Xi sighed, consumed with emotion after saying this. He suddenly appeared older than he seemed. “You’re the first one I’ve ever told this story to, the only one who is worthy of hearing it.”

Chen Chen eyed the professor suspiciously upon hearing this. “Based on what you’re telling me, it seems like you’re on this energy form being’s side. Are you telling me that you’re one of the survivors of its race?”

“I am, but I also am not.”

Wang Xi chuckled softly. “I was once a common human born in a village in a mountainous region, it’s name I no longer remember. One day, I happened to encounter a consciousness fragment of C’tan and my mind merged with it. From that moment on, eons of memories tracking back to the beginning of time including the great war became part of me as well. I’m now Wang Xi, but I am also C’tan...”

“Right.”

Wang Xi explained, “C’tan is the name which the cursed civilization refers to me by. It’s the first name that I’ve ever received, but now I have another name that I go by...

“Void Dragon!”

When Wang Xi uttered the name “Void Dragon”, Chen Chen suddenly felt that all the hair on his body rose on its end and felt a malicious energy rushing at him. Without any hesitation, Chen Chen unleashed an enormous surge of power from the depths of his soul!

“Boom!”

The otherworldly powers collided and burst like two tsunamis crashing into each other. The concentration of both sources of power was condensed to an extreme molecular level and ground into each other like gears of a giant spinning wheel opposing each other. The collision was tremendous, but the environment around them seemed scarcely affected!

“What terrific, pure strength!”

Wang Xi was impressed and celebrated. “You’ve indeed gained the power of C’tan, even if it’s still incomplete as of yet. You’ve only mastered one of the four basic forces in the universe, but your body holds two different powers coexisting with each other, truly incredible! I thank you, Chen Chen. You’ve carved a path of Godhood for me and helped me realize that our powers can be combined with the psionic powers!”

“Godhood?”

Chen Chen’s smile was full of mockery. “You want to become one of those three ridiculous beings in the subspace?”

“Of course!”

Wang Xi emitted traces of green glow around him, his skin began to assume a metallic texture. “They were responsible for arousing hatred among my species in the past. What I plan to do is to adopt mankind into the psionic era and this way, strengthen the ties between mankind and the subspace. From there on, I’ll crush mankind and assume my role as the one and only true ‘God’, an omniscient being that will surpass those three!

“I’d show you some mercy and let you live considering your many contributions to my goal but... It seems like you have other plans in mind and are trying to steer mankind away from the subspace. Such a shame...”

Chen Chen retorted icily, “You were the one who told me there was no such thing as a God.”

“If I didn’t tell you that, wouldn’t you harbor some foolish ideas of your own when you confront those three beings in the subspace?”

The green glow around Wang Xi became brighter and the metal shells on his skin grew more defined with each passing second. Suddenly, a thick mechanical tail emerged from behind his back!

Wang Xi’s face was now covered with a metallic black mask. A harsh, metallic sound zoned in on Chen Chen from all directions. “But I wasn’t lying either because there are no other beings that can claim to be God apart from me!”

“No. There’s no God nor will there ever be.”

Chen Chen spat, “And you’re no exception!”

“Bang!”

Once again, an indescribable power erupted from Chen Chen’s body. Moments after that, a faint yellow ripple in reality formed between Chen Chen and Professor Wang Xi as if an invisible glass was shattering in the space between them. A breath of subspace current set to destroy everything in its path seeped out of the crack!

“Subspace rift?”

Wang Xi’s voice was no longer humanoid but sounded mechanical, mixed with a metallic hum. Despite that, there was a sharp sense of shock and awe concealed within his voice. “Your psionic strength has already reached a level strong enough to tear apart reality and access the subspace?”

Chen Chen did not respond to this with his words but with his subsequent actions instead. Mountains worth of psionic energy surged around his surroundings and threatened to overwhelm Wang Xi. A brazen gravitational pull came from below Wang Xi, powerful enough to shred through reality, and in the next second, Wang Xi was flung into the gap leading to the subspace!

“You’re too naive if you think you can simply banish me like this!”

Wang Xi belted with a quick motion and summoned countless black shapes from his body. Chen Chen’s expression suddenly changed when he made out what those things were!

They were... Black slates!

One, two, three, ten, fifty, ninety-nine!

Chen Chen could not believe the sheer number of black slates he was seeing. He saw the endless black slates form a dark wall of steel and walled off the tidal force of the subspace!

“Bang!”

The subspace rift was forcefully sealed!

“So many Civilization Monoliths...”

Chen Chen’s eyes were colored with wonder. “This explains why most of the dimensions we traveled to are already destroyed...”

“I was the one who granted you the dimension keys. Naturally, I can use them as well, sooner than you could even.”

Wang Xi made a quick gesture and materialized a glowing USB drive in the center of his palms. It turned into a glowing orb, then continued to rotate between different shapes and forms without stopping. “This is the ultimate invention of the mentor civilization, the creations that allow for endless dimension, space, and time travel. How could you possibly expect me to fully submit such a powerful tool to your hands?”

After Wang Xi mentioned this, Chen Chen suddenly sensed an unimaginable weight pressing over his psionic core. He could not evoke the faintest trace of psionic power as they were completely suppressed by the psionic suppressing characteristic of the wall of black slate!

“You call them Civilization Monolith, but the cursed civilization refers to them by another name... the Blackstone Array!”

Wang Xi’s voice grew harsher and colder as a pair of metallic wings tore through his flesh and appeared behind his back. A strange symbol levitated in front of his forehead while he emitted a ripple of power similar to Field. “The reality stability anchor you use is nothing but a simplified replica of the Blackstone Array the cursed civilization used to control the subspace!”

chapters.title: Chapter 643: Righteous Indignation
chapters.body:
Chapter 643: Righteous Indignation

Chapter 643: Righteous Indignation

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

Lu Yubing was a student at Jiaotong University.

Today was just like any other day where she got up in the morning, showered, put on minimal makeup, and made herself ready for the day. The first lecture today was led by Professor Wang Xi who was a senior academician of the Chinese Academy of Sciences, an esteemed member of the biological world, but...

Upon stepping into the classroom, Lu Yubing did a double-take when she saw the old lecturer standing by the podium.

Wang Xi?

Who was Wang Xi? Oh right, senior academician of the Chinese Academy of Sciences, her biology class lecturer.

However...

Lu Yubing frowned in her seat. Her classmates chatted around her while she sensed a strange cloud looming over her.

There was no mistaking it. She knew who Wang Xi was, however... There seemed to be no recollection of this professor in her mind whatsoever.

However, it seemed like none of the other students in the class were fazed by the appearance of the lecturer. This observation quickly cast away whatever lingering suspicions she had in her mind. Perhaps her mind was just feeling absent today?

Lu Yubing shook her head a few times to wring off the distressed expression. Maybe all this studying was getting to her head. How could she not remember the well-respected Professor Wang Xi...

The lecture session was forty-five minutes long. Lu Yubing started listening to the lecturer and quickly began zoning out, her trance was suddenly interrupted by the class bell ringing.

“Lu, class is over, what are you staring at?”

A petite girl appeared beside Lu Yubing and waved several times to get her attention, then teased her, “Are you thinking about the hottie in the other class?”

“What are you talking about?”

Lu Yubing quickly blushed and stammered, “Come on Hui, don’t start any strange rumors... I was just thinking that Professor Wang Xi had been teaching us for the better half of the semester but for some reason, he still feels very alien to me...”

“Hm?”

The petite girl Lu Yubing addressed as Hui jerked slightly as she heard this. “Now that you mentioned it, I do get the same impression.”

The two exchanged a look. Lu Yubing was about to state something else that came to mind but Hui grabbed her and spoke first, “Alright, it’s probably just all in our heads. Who cares right? Let’s go to the library before all the good seats are taken!”

Just like that, Lu Yubing evacuated from the lecture hall with her friend.

Moments after stepping out of the lecture hall, she stopped again, this time afflicted with another queer sensation.

Earlier, there was nothing in between her and the door, but why did she feel compelled to walk around an invisible obstacle?

It seemed as if someone was standing in the middle of the path and both she and Hui instinctively knew to get out of that person’s way. The problem was... Nobody was standing there...

Lu Yubing shuddered uncontrollably.

“What’s the matter with you?”

Hui noticed Lu Yubing acting strangely and turned to her with a concerned expression. “Are you feeling feverish? You’ve been zoning out a lot today.”

“No, it’s nothing...”

Lu Yubing shook her head and brushed it off. She stole one last look at the classroom door and noticed that it was shut without her realizing it...

Although she was still feeling slightly unnerved, she decided that there was no need to overthink it and proceeded to leave with Hui.

It would appear that today was destined to be more than any ordinary day. The moment Lu Yubing and Hui left the lecture building and arrived at the library, Lu Yubing suddenly felt a terrible throb in the center of her heart. She could not identify the source of this sudden sensation. It was as if a demon king had descended to reality or perhaps a rift had been forcefully torn open into time and space. All she knew was that it left a deep, scarring impression on her!

Lu Yubing subconsciously turned in the direction of the lecture building and witnessed a sight that she would never forget for the rest of her life – a ripple in between the seams of reality, a wholly unfathomable sight, appeared above the building. It was as if reality were split cleanly in half with a sharp blade. The rift emitted a faint yellow-ray within and declared itself with an ominous presence!

The moment she witnessed this bizarre happening, the people around her all clutched their heads with their arms and screamed in pain!

“Lu, my head hurts, it hurts so much!”

Hui screamed in agony. She was in such overwhelming pain that her sharp nails like claws dug into her flesh without herself even realizing it!

“Hui!”

Lu Yubing was horrified by this sight and tried to brush off the throbbing pain in her head and help her friend. She carried Hui into the library and realized that the pain subsided ever so slightly after entering the sheltered space of the library.

Despite the subsided pain, there was still an overbearing presence cycling inside the depths of her mind, a presence that had a terrible hold on her.

“What in the world is that?”

The people in the library slowly recollected themselves and looked outside the windows, each one of them pointed in the direction of the lecture building. The reality rift above the building vanished as abruptly as it appeared but moments after that, a bolt of green lightning burst out of the building!

The bolt of green lightning was unlike anything any of them had ever witnessed. It seemed to possess a life of its own and continuously spread and expand its web-like pattern outward. Everything it came into contact with was instantly vaporized into the barest molecular form!

They heard the concrete structure of the building crumbling around them!

In a split instant, the entire lecture building was vaporized into a cloud of dust. Amid the formation of smoke and dust were two faint figures.

Lu Yubing approached the window and looked outside, petrified with fear. She saw the two figures levitating mid-air. One of which was a figure completely covered in metallic shells and had a metallic wing as well as a tail growing out its back. This was the entity that produced the Armageddon bolt of lightning. It was a divine entity that descended into the realm of mankind!

The other figure that seemed to oppose it was an ordinary human figure. With one simple glance, Lu Yubing could tell that this seemingly ordinary human figure was no mere mortal. How could an otherwise regular person stand amid the ever-expanding lightning storm and survive the instant vaporization of the entire building?

“Holy crap, what or who is that?”

“Is it a robot? The terminator? Is this Dragonball?”

“Get out of here! Those are two psionicists battling it out!”

“Let’s get out of here! That one who controls lightning demolished the entire lecture building in an instant. There’s no way this building can do any better!”

Somebody cried out and alerted everyone in the library. Everyone shared the same sentiment and began to panic and scramble toward the back exit of the library!

“Lu, let’s go!”

Hui cried out and followed the rest to the exit.

“Hui! Hui!”

Lu Yubing called after her friend and chased after her heels. Somewhere along the lines, the previous haunting feeling in her chest faded away but was replaced with another variant of heavy sensation like a boulder was placed over her chest. The more she ran, the harder it became to breathe.

“Boom!”

As soon as Lu Yubing made it out of the library, her ears were filled with the mighty crash of an earth-shattering explosion. The sky above was transformed into a sheet of dazzling green from the glow of the bolt of lightning!

The ground below her feet quaked like the Earth was being pulled apart in all directions!

Was it even possible for humans to achieve such great feats of destruction?

The thought crossed Lu Yubing’s terrified mind.

“Run!”

“Go! Go! Go!”

“Help, can anybody save us!”

Lu Yubing found herself surrounded by harrowing cries of desperation all around her. Some people could not find the strength to even move their feet anymore and knelt on the ground helplessly. She wanted to help them but suddenly, the library was blown apart, and sent gigantic blocks of projectile concrete crashing into the crowd. With her very own eyes, she saw some of them crushed under the weight of the boulder!

“Ah!”

More shattered concrete rained around her. She winced in fear but knew that she had to continue running. Every single thought in her mind in this instant was silenced. The only thing she understood at this moment was that she had to get as far away from here as possible!

More deafening noises filled her ears and more projectiles crashed all around her. The destruction transformed the entire compound into a hellscape and when she finally ran out of strength to run, she looked around herself to see that the entire schoolyard was a ruin of concrete and the occasional splattered blood and body remnants.

“Boom!”

While Lu Yubing’s mind was arrested with shock, she heard a mighty crash some distance behind her that made her jump. She sensed a presence behind her that was not as powerful as the previous presence she felt.

Lu Yubing fearfully turned around to see a dark-haired young man with darker eyes lying on the ground. His body was in horrible shape. Meanwhile, a metallic figure slowly descended from the sky above, emitting dazzling glows of green light around its silhouette. Its presence was sinister...

“Chen Chen, you’ve lost.”

The metallic figure seemed to speak by creating deep vibrations in the air around it. As alien as it appeared, it spoke perfect Chinese, “You who have had your psionic power suppressed and have only obtained a tiny quarter of my species are no match for me. This power you hold so much esteem in can only be compared to that of an infant.”

“I’ve lost?”

The young man on the ground made a valiant effort of getting up with one arm as the other arm was severed. He then grimaced. “You think you’ve defeated me?”

After that, sprouts of flesh emerged from the torn flesh that once held the man’s severed arm. They crawled and intertwined with each other to form shapes to repair the man’s body. The sheer sight of it made Lu Yubing’s skin crawl.

“You’re too naive if you think that your inventions can save you.”

The metallic figure remarked coldly, then waved his hand slightly and called the bolts of green lightning in the air, and beckoned them to his command. In an instant, they burst with a mighty explosion like a firework!

Amid the thunderous crash, enormous battle spaceships appeared in the sky above!

Lu Yubing looked up, the horror had become a permanent fixture in her eyes at this point. She did not know how the spaceship appeared out of nowhere or perhaps it had been camouflaged in the sky above all along. However, their appearance only lasted a brief moment because the entire fleet of spaceships was blown apart by the bolts of lightning in an instant!

The city that was already largely devastated by this point was once again assaulted by a rain of falling components of the destroyed spaceship. It set off explosions of various magnitudes all over the city and plunged the city into a sea of flames!

“Chen Chen, do you truly believe that earthly technologies could pose any harm for someone like me?”

The sky burned and the figure stood amid the fiery destruction, completely unaffected. Its clear and deep voice continued traveling through the air and reaching the ones below. “Since the birth of our species, we’ve seen countless civilizations, many of which are more powerful than mankind. We’ve seen them harness the power of nuclear fusion combined with antimatter and vacuum zero-point energy. At least those civilizations may pose some slight problems for us but human beings? Human beings are simply too primitive compared to them and hardly worth our time...”

“Heh, hehe...”

Despite having his fleet wiped out in an instant, the dark-haired young man chuckled at his predicament. In time, he broke into a burst of terrific laughter!

“Ah ha ha ha ha ha ha!!!”

“What are you laughing about?”

The metallic figure hovering above was taken aback by this sudden outburst of laughter from the young man. “If this is your idea of buying time, I have to say that I expected better of you.

“Wang Xi, there’s one thing that you forgot.”

The intimidating response of the dark-haired young man sent shivers down Lu Yubing’s spine. “Of course, it’s a simple task for you to kill me right here and now but don’t forget that I have my artificial intelligence assistant. The moment I die, my assistant will detonate the nuclear weapons deposited around the world and simultaneously bring an end to the Dyson cloud project still under development. This will trigger a never-before-seen solar storm that will take all of mankind with me to the grave!

“This way, your plans are crippled as well. Your dreams of achieving Godhood will turn to dust!”

What?

Lu Yubing looked at the dark-haired young man who had just uttered the most inconceivable sentence she had ever heard in her lifetime. This devil intended to bring all of mankind down with him? Could the metallic figure above be the savior of mankind while this human was the villain? Was this what this battle was about?

Hold on, this metallic figure built like an iron soldier was Professor Wang Xi?

“What?”

Even the metallic figure was taken aback by this declaration. The surge of electricity around him seemed to stop as well while he puzzled over the threat. If the metallic figure had anything resembling human expression, it would undoubtedly be one of great confusion now.

“Do you have any idea what you’re saying?”

The metallic figure stopped for a moment and rebuked harshly, “Chen Chen, I know you better than you know yourself! I was the one who gave you a lesson on the sanctity of mankind and the pursuit of righteousness... How could someone like you who claims to be a guardian of mankind intend to bring it down with you in the moment of your death?”

“Why would I care about all that if I’m going to die anyway?”

A cruel flash appeared in the young man’s eyes. He bore a smile with two rows of pearly white teeth exposed. “Come on, kill me! It’s now or never!”

Faced with this difficult decision, the metallic figure suddenly hesitated.

“Kill him! Now!”

While the metallic figure was still puzzling over the difficult dilemma, Lu Yubing could not sit by idly as a silent observer anymore and screamed at the top of her voice, “Hurry up and kill him! I’d rather mankind die than to have our lives held hostage by a villain like him!”

Lu Yubing held back her tears and shouted, “All of my classmates died and our city was destroyed without repair. If you don’t kill him now, you’re desecrating the innocent lives lost!”

“...”

Both the metallic figure and the dark-haired young man looked in Lu Yubing’s direction in unison, then exchanged an odd look with each other. There was not a word exchanged between them.

All of a sudden, the atmosphere became quite awkward.

chapters.title: Chapter 644: Nothing Like A Free Meal
chapters.body:
